english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Philippians

verses.latin_vulgate: Itaque fratres mei carissimi, et desideratissimi, gaudium meum, et corona mea: sic state in Domino, carissimi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥστε ἀδελφοί μου ἀγαπητοὶ καὶ ἐπιπόθητοι χαρὰ καὶ στέφανός μου οὕτως στήκετε ἐν κυρίῳ ἀγαπητοί
verses.king_james: Therefore, my brethren dearly beloved and longed for, my joy and crown, so stand fast in the Lord, *my* dearly beloved.

verses.latin_vulgate: Evodiam rogo, et Syntychen deprecor, idipsum sapere in Domino.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὐωδίαν παρακαλῶ καὶ συντύχην παρακαλῶ τὸ αὐτὸ φρονεῖν ἐν κυρίῳ
verses.king_james: I beseech Euodias, and beseech Syntyche, that they be of the same mind in the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Etiam rogo et te, germane compar, adjuva illas, quæ mecum laboraverunt in Evangelio cum Clemente, et ceteris adjutoribus meis, quorum nomina sunt in libro vitæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐρωτῶ καὶ σέ σύζυγε γνήσιε συλλαμβάνου αὐταῖς αἵτινες ἐν τῷ εὐαγγελίῳ συνήθλησάν μοι μετὰ καὶ κλήμεντος καὶ τῶν λοιπῶν συνεργῶν μου ὧν τὰ ὀνόματα ἐν βίβλῳ ζωῆς
verses.king_james: And I intreat thee also, true yokefellow, help those women which laboured with me in the gospel, with Clement also, and *with* other my fellowlabourers, whose names *are* in the book of life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gaudete in Domino semper: iterum dico gaudete.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: χαίρετε ἐν κυρίῳ πάντοτε πάλιν ἐρῶ χαίρετε
verses.king_james: Rejoice in the Lord alway: *and* again I say, Rejoice.

verses.latin_vulgate: Modestia vestra nota sit omnibus hominibus: Dominus prope est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ ἐπιεικὲς ὑμῶν γνωσθήτω πᾶσιν ἀνθρώποις ὁ κύριος ἐγγύς
verses.king_james: Let your moderation be known unto all men. The Lord *is* at hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nihil solliciti sitis: sed in omni oratione, et obsecratione, cum gratiarum actione petitiones vestræ innotescant apud Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μηδὲν μεριμνᾶτε ἀλλ᾽ ἐν παντὶ τῇ προσευχῇ καὶ τῇ δεήσει μετὰ εὐχαριστίας τὰ αἰτήματα ὑμῶν γνωριζέσθω πρὸς τὸν θεόν
verses.king_james: Be careful for nothing; but in every thing by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et pax Dei, quæ exuperat omnem sensum, custodiat corda vestra, et intelligentias vestras in Christo Jesu.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἡ εἰρήνη τοῦ θεοῦ ἡ ὑπερέχουσα πάντα νοῦν φρουρήσει τὰς καρδίας ὑμῶν καὶ τὰ νοήματα ὑμῶν ἐν χριστῷ ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: And the peace of God, which passeth all understanding, shall keep your hearts and minds through Christ Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: De cetero fratres, quæcumque sunt vera, quæcumque pudica, quæcumque justa, quæcumque sancta, quæcumque amabilia, quæcumque bonæ famæ, siqua virtus, siqua laus disciplinæ, hæc cogitate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ λοιπόν ἀδελφοί ὅσα ἐστὶν ἀληθῆ ὅσα σεμνά ὅσα δίκαια ὅσα ἁγνά ὅσα προσφιλῆ ὅσα εὔφημα εἴ τις ἀρετὴ καὶ εἴ τις ἔπαινος ταῦτα λογίζεσθε
verses.king_james: Finally, brethren, whatsoever things are true, whatsoever things *are* honest, whatsoever things *are* just, whatsoever things *are* pure, whatsoever things *are* lovely, whatsoever things *are* of good report; if *there be* any virtue, and if *there be* any praise, think on these things.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ et didicistis, et accepistis, et audistis, et vidistis in me, hæc agite: et Deus pacis erit vobiscum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἃ καὶ ἐμάθετε καὶ παρελάβετε καὶ ἠκούσατε καὶ εἴδετε ἐν ἐμοί ταῦτα πράσσετε καὶ ὁ θεὸς τῆς εἰρήνης ἔσται μεθ᾽ ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: Those things, which ye have both learned, and received, and heard, and seen in me, do: and the God of peace shall be with you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gavisus sum autem in Domino vehementer, quoniam tandem aliquando refloruistis pro me sentire, sicut et sentiebatis: occupati autem eratis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐχάρην δὲ ἐν κυρίῳ μεγάλως ὅτι ἤδη ποτὲ ἀνεθάλετε τὸ ὑπὲρ ἐμοῦ φρονεῖν ἐφ᾽ ᾧ καὶ ἐφρονεῖτε ἠκαιρεῖσθε δέ
verses.king_james: But I rejoiced in the Lord greatly, that now at the last your care of me hath flourished again; wherein ye were also careful, but ye lacked opportunity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non quasi propter penuriam dico: ego enim didici, in quibus sum, sufficiens esse.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐχ ὅτι καθ᾽ ὑστέρησιν λέγω ἐγὼ γὰρ ἔμαθον ἐν οἷς εἰμι αὐτάρκης εἶναι
verses.king_james: Not that I speak in respect of want: for I have learned, in whatsoever state I am, *therewith* to be content.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scio et humiliari, scio et abundare (ubique et in omnibus institutus sum): et satiari, et esurire, et abundare, et penuriam pati.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἶδα δὲ ταπεινοῦσθαι οἶδα καὶ περισσεύειν ἐν παντὶ καὶ ἐν πᾶσιν μεμύημαι καὶ χορτάζεσθαι καὶ πεινᾶν καὶ περισσεύειν καὶ ὑστερεῖσθαι
verses.king_james: I know both how to be abased, and I know how to abound: every where and in all things I am instructed both to be full and to be hungry, both to abound and to suffer need.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnia possum in eo qui me confortat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάντα ἰσχύω ἐν τῷ ἐνδυναμοῦντί με χριστῷ
verses.king_james: I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verumtamen bene fecistis, communicantes tribulationi meæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πλὴν καλῶς ἐποιήσατε συγκοινωνήσαντές μου τῇ θλίψει
verses.king_james: Notwithstanding ye have well done, that ye did communicate with my affliction.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scitis autem et vos Philippenses, quod in principio Evangelii, quando profectus sum a Macedonia, nulla mihi ecclesia communicavit in ratione dati et accepti, nisi vos soli:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἴδατε δὲ καὶ ὑμεῖς φιλιππήσιοι ὅτι ἐν ἀρχῇ τοῦ εὐαγγελίου ὅτε ἐξῆλθον ἀπὸ μακεδονίας οὐδεμία μοι ἐκκλησία ἐκοινώνησεν εἰς λόγον δόσεως καὶ λήψεως, εἰ μὴ ὑμεῖς μόνοι
verses.king_james: Now ye Philippians know also, that in the beginning of the gospel, when I departed from Macedonia, no church communicated with me as concerning giving and receiving, but ye only.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia et Thessalonicam semel et bis in usum mihi misistis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι καὶ ἐν φεσσαλονίκῃ καὶ ἅπαξ καὶ δὶς εἰς τὴν χρείαν μοι ἐπέμψατε
verses.king_james: For even in Thessalonica ye sent once and again unto my necessity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non quia quæro datum, sed requiro fructum abundantem in ratione vestra.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐχ ὅτι ἐπιζητῶ τὸ δόμα ἀλλ᾽ ἐπιζητῶ τὸν καρπὸν τὸν πλεονάζοντα εἰς λόγον ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: Not because I desire a gift: but I desire fruit that may abound to your account.

verses.latin_vulgate: Habeo autem omnia, et abundo: repletus sum, acceptis ab Epaphrodito quæ misistis odorem suavitatis, hostiam acceptam, placentem Deo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπέχω δὲ πάντα καὶ περισσεύω πεπλήρωμαι δεξάμενος παρὰ ἐπαφροδίτου τὰ παρ᾽ ὑμῶν ὀσμὴν εὐωδίας θυσίαν δεκτήν εὐάρεστον τῷ θεῷ
verses.king_james: But I have all, and abound: I am full, having received of Epaphroditus the things *which were sent* from you, an odour of a sweet smell, a sacrifice acceptable, wellpleasing to God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus autem meus impleat omne desiderium vestrum secundum divitias suas in gloria in Christo Jesu.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ θεός μου πληρώσει πᾶσαν χρείαν ὑμῶν κατὰ τὸν πλοῦτον αὐτοῦ ἐν δόξῃ ἐν χριστῷ ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: But my God shall supply all your need according to his riches in glory by Christ Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deo autem et Patri nostro gloria in sæcula sæculorum. Amen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῷ δὲ θεῷ καὶ πατρὶ ἡμῶν ἡ δόξα εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας τῶν αἰώνων ἀμήν
verses.king_james: Now unto God and our Father *be* glory for ever and ever. Amen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutate omnem sanctum in Christo Jesu.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάσασθε πάντα ἅγιον ἐν χριστῷ ἰησοῦ ἀσπάζονται ὑμᾶς οἱ σὺν ἐμοὶ ἀδελφοί
verses.king_james: Salute every saint in Christ Jesus. The brethren which are with me greet you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutant vos, qui mecum sunt, fratres. Salutant vos omnes sancti, maxime autem qui de Cæsaris domo sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάζονται ὑμᾶς πάντες οἱ ἅγιοι μάλιστα δὲ οἱ ἐκ τῆς καίσαρος οἰκίας
verses.king_james: All the saints salute you, chiefly they that are of Caesar's household.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gratia Domini nostri Jesu Christi cum spiritu vestro. Amen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ χάρις τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ μετὰ πάντων ὑμῶν ἀμην
verses.king_james: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ *be* with you all. Amen.
